back: Hidden standing instructions inserted by the app before your message - the model's job description and much of its personality.
The message you actually write - your request to the model.
Everything else the model can see: earlier messages, pasted documents, tool results. What the model sees is all it knows.
Including a few examples of the desired output in your prompt so the model copies the pattern. Show, don't tell.
Prompting the model to reason step by step before answering. Puts the working-out on the page and boosts accuracy on hard problems.
The craft of designing, testing, and refining prompts systematically - technical writing for a very literal reader.
An attack where malicious text (e.g. hidden in a webpage) tricks the model into ignoring its instructions.
Filters and rules around a model that constrain what it can say or be talked into - a defense against misuse and injection.
